tool_args.label: Key Performance Metrics Report October 2022
tool_args.raw_text: LONG ISLAND RAIL ROAD & METRO-NORTH RAILROAD KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS October 2022

payload.title: LONG ISLAND RAIL ROAD & METRO-NORTH RAILROAD KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
payload.description: Performance metrics document prepared for the October 2022 meeting of the Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committees

evidence_refs.source_quote: LONG ISLAND RAIL ROAD & METRO-NORTH RAILROAD KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
October 2022
This performance metrics document was prepared for the October 2022 meeting of the Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committees.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Long Island Rail Road
tool_args.raw_text: Long Island Rail Road is comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.

payload.description: Over 7,000 employees, serving over 150,000 passengers a day, operating 700 trains daily, maintaining 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Long Island Rail Road is comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.

tool_args.label: Metro-North Railroad
tool_args.raw_text: Metro-North Railroad is comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

payload.description: Nearly 6,000 employees, serving over 150,000 passengers a day, operating 700 trains daily, maintaining 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Metro-North Railroad is comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

tool_args.raw_text: Lieutenant Governor Antonio Delgado

evidence_refs.source_quote: Lieutenant Governor Antonio Delgado

tool_args.label: 3rd Track Constructors (3TC)
tool_args.raw_text: design-build contract led by 3rd Track Constructors (3TC), a consortium of design and construction firms

payload.description: Consortium of design and construction firms serving as design-build contractor for LIRR Third Track project

evidence_refs.source_quote: design-build contract led by 3rd Track Constructors (3TC), a consortium of design and construction firms

tool_args.label: LIRR Main Line Third Track
tool_args.raw_text: The third and final section of the LIRR Main Line Third Track between Carle Place and Hicksville opened on Monday, October 3

payload.description: 9.8 miles of new third track on the LIRR Main Line between Carle Place and Hicksville. Completed on October 3, 2022. The project opened on time and $100 million under budget. Enables a 41% systemwide service increase when Grand Central Madison opens.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The third and final section of the LIRR Main Line Third Track between Carle Place and Hicksville opened on Monday, October 3

This region-shaping project was delivered on-time and nearly $100 million under budget. The new 9.8-mile track will enable a 41% systemwide service increase when Grand Central Madison opens later this year.

tool_args.label: Grand Central Madison
tool_args.raw_text: Grand Central Madison service before the end of the year

payload.description: New LIRR terminal at Grand Central Terminal. Service expected before the end of 2022. Pre-revenue testing underway with eight trains simultaneously. Crews conducting physical characteristics training. Will enable 41% service increase in conjunction with LIRR Third Track.

evidence_refs.source_quote: With Grand Central Madison service coming into clearer view, crews are performing safety and security drills.
unlocking huge benefits for LIRR customers and operations, especially in conjunction with the opening of Grand Central Madison service before the end of the year.

tool_args.label: Elmont-UBS Arena Station Two-Way Service
tool_args.raw_text: celebrated the opening of two-way service at LIRR's Elmont-UBS Arena Station. This is the first new LIRR station in almost 50 years.

payload.description: First new LIRR station in almost 50 years. Now accommodates both eastbound and westbound trains on event days. Full-time, year-round service to begin by end of 2022.

evidence_refs.source_quote: celebrated the opening of two-way service at LIRR's Elmont-UBS Arena Station. This is the first new LIRR station in almost 50 years.
Elmont-UBS Arena Station can now accommodate both eastbound and westbound trains on event days and LIRR will begin full-time, year-round service by the end of 2022.

tool_args.label: Park Avenue Viaduct Replacement
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development issued a Request for Proposals (RFP) for the partial replacement and repair of Metro-North Railroad's Park Avenue Viaduct.

payload.description: Partial replacement and repair of Metro-North Railroad's Park Avenue Viaduct. RFP issued. Design-build contract award on track for later this year. Major construction expected to begin fall 2023. Repaired viaduct anticipated in service in 2027. Work between East 115 Street and East 132 Street.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Construction & Development issued a Request for Proposals (RFP) for the partial replacement and repair of Metro-North Railroad's Park Avenue Viaduct.

Award of the design-build construction contract is on track for as soon as later this year. In the fall of 2023, major construction is expected to begin, and the repaired viaduct is anticipated to be in service in 2027.

tool_args.label: MTA First-Mile/Last-Mile Initiative
tool_args.raw_text: MTA launched a first-mile/last-mile initiative last year to collaborate with local stakeholders who wish to promote alternative ways of traveling to our stations.

payload.description: Initiative launched to collaborate with local stakeholders to promote alternative ways of traveling to LIRR and Metro-North stations. Includes interactive online toolkit, station assessments, and pilot programs in ten communities across LIRR and Metro-North territories.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA launched a first-mile/last-mile initiative last year to collaborate with local stakeholders who wish to promote those alternative ways of traveling to our stations.
MTA is partnering with ten interested communities across our seven-county commuter rail territory to complete station assessments and design first-mile/last-mile pilot programs

issues: evidence_refs[1].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: Metro-North Laser Train Technology Adoption
tool_args.raw_text: Metro-North's adoption of laser train technology for the first time this year. Pioneered by LIRR in 2018.

payload.description: Metro-North adopting laser train technology pioneered by LIRR in 2018. Uses high-intensity lasers to remove leaf debris from tracks. Metro-North will pilot it at higher speeds. High-pressure rail-washer trains also used by both railroads.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Metro-North's adoption of laser train technology for the first time this year. Pioneered by LIRR in 2018
For the first time, Metro-North will be using laser cleaning technology to remove slippery pectin residue on rails caused by falling leaves.

tool_args.label: MTA First-Mile/Last-Mile Initiative
tool_args.raw_text: MTA launched a first-mile/last-mile initiative last year to collaborate with local stakeholders who wish to promote alternative ways of traveling to our stations.

payload.description: Initiative launched to collaborate with local stakeholders to promote alternative ways of traveling to LIRR and Metro-North stations. Includes interactive online toolkit, station assessments, and pilot programs in ten communities across LIRR and Metro-North territories.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA launched a first-mile/last-mile initiative last year to collaborate with local stakeholders who wish to promote those alternative ways of traveling to our stations.
MTA is partnering with ten interested communities across our seven-county commuter rail territory to complete station assessments and design first-mile/last-mile pilot programs

payload.description: Governor Hochul presiding at a ribbon-cutting ceremony in Westbury to open the third and final section of the LIRR Main Line Third Track between Carle Place and Hicksville.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The third and final section of the LIRR Main Line Third Track between Carle Place and Hicksville opened on Monday, October 3, with Governor Hochul presiding at a ribbon-cutting ceremony in Westbury.
